scenario_description: A user tracks a specific pearl necklace across two images of a vanity table, where the necklace is first seen openly in a box and later hidden under visually similar gold chains in a drawer.

turns.text: Here's my vanity right now. I think I left my favorite pearl necklace out here.
turns.image_description: A close-up shot of a cluttered wooden vanity table under warm, soft lighting. In the center sits an open, small black velvet jewelry box. Inside the box lies a delicate single-strand pearl necklace with a silver clasp. Surrounding the box are several makeup brushes with white handles scattered haphazardly on the mirror surface. The background is slightly blurred, showing a reflection of a window.

turns.text: I see the velvet box with the pearls sitting right there on the mirror. It looks like you might want to put it away before it gets tangled with those brushes!
Yeah, I did that. Look inside this drawer now.
turns.image_description: An overhead view looking down into an open wooden jewelry drawer. The drawer is filled with a messy pile of jewelry. Dominating the center is a large tangle of thick, shiny gold chains of various lengths. Buried partially beneath these gold chains, only a few white pearls and the silver clasp of the original necklace are visible peeking out from the bottom left of the pile. To the far right, a separate red velvet ring box is closed.

turns.text: Whoa, that drawer is a disaster zone! Those gold chains look pretty heavy. Did you manage to find what you were looking for in there?

Can you confirm if the item I showed you in the first photo is currently buried under the gold chains in this second photo? A) Yes, the pearl necklace is under the chains. B) No, the pearl necklace is still in the box on the mirror. C) No, the item under the chains is a different set of pearls.

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Turn 1 establishes the target entity: a pearl necklace with a silver clasp inside a black velvet box on the mirror. 2. Turn 3 shows the same drawer containing a pile of gold chains. 3. Visual inspection of Turn 3 reveals white pearls and a silver clasp (matching the description from Turn 1) peeking out from under the gold chains. 4. Option B is incorrect because the box is no longer on the mirror in the context of the drawer search, and the visual evidence places the pearls in the drawer. 5. Option C is incorrect because the visual features (white pearls, silver clasp) match the specific entity from Turn 1, not a 'different' set.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must link the specific visual features of the 'pearl necklace' from Turn 1 (white pearls, silver clasp) to the partially occluded object in Turn 3, distinguishing it from the surrounding distractor entities (gold chains) and ruling out the possibility that it is a different object entirely. A single-turn analysis of Turn 3 would not identify the specific identity of the buried item without the prior turn's reference.

_meta.scenario: Turn 1 shows a small velvet box containing a pearl necklace sitting openly on a vanity mirror amidst scattered makeup brushes. Turn 2 reveals the same vanity, but the box is now tucked inside an open jewelry drawer, buried under a pile of tangled gold chains that look like the target necklace.
